sample.description: What this profile is actually based on. Keeps the analysis honest about its evidence.

original_vs_amplified.description: Rough split of original tweets vs retweets/replies, e.g. '70% original, 30% RT/reply'.

note.description: Any sampling caveat, e.g. 'only most recent 800 tweets', 'API returned partial window'.

how_they_think.description: The mind behind the account, not just the metrics.

worldview.description: One-paragraph synthesis of how they see the world.

evidence_quote.description: A verbatim tweet that shows this model in action.

confidence.description: 0-1: how strongly the corpus supports attributing this belief.

reasoning_style.description: How they argue: first-principles, empirical, contrarian, analogical, narrative, data-driven, vibes, etc.

tensions_or_blind_spots.description: Internal contradictions, topics they dodge, or unexamined assumptions.

register.description: Overall tone: formal/casual, earnest/ironic, warm/combative, etc.

device.description: e.g. lowercase, em-dash asides, one-liners, numbered threads, rhetorical questions, dunking, greentext.

example.description: A verbatim tweet showing the device.

vocabulary.description: Characteristic words, phrases, and coinages they reach for.

sentence_patterns.description: Typical sentence length, rhythm, punctuation habits.

emoji_and_formatting.description: Emoji use, capitalization, line breaks, thread structure.

imitation_prompt.description: A ready-to-use system prompt that would let an AI write a tweet AS this person. Optional payload for voice-matching use cases.

weight.description: 0-1 share of attention this topic gets in the corpus.

relationship.description: e.g. amplifies, argues with, cites as authority, mentors, mocks.

dynamics.description: The longitudinal view — how the account has changed across the sample window. This is the differentiated part; only fill it when timestamps support it.

trajectory.description: Narrative of how the account evolved over the window. Empty string if the corpus has no usable time span.

approx_when.description: Rough date or period the shift happened.

fading.description: Topics they used to post about and have dropped.

summary.description: Executive paragraph a human can read in 20 seconds.

confidence.description: 0-1 overall confidence given corpus size and quality.

notes.description: Data limitations, caveats, anything that would change the read with more data.
